title: Didelta cernua
taxonomy: Didelta cernua is classified in the family Asteraceae. The genus Didelta is part of the tribe Mutisieae within this family. The species D. cernua is native to South Africa. It is a flowering plant that belongs to the class Magnoliopsida. The full classification of Didelta cernua includes kingdom Plantae.

intro: Didelta cernua, also known as the drooping Didelta or climbing Didelta, is a plant species belonging to the Asteraceae family. The plant has been traditionally used to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and expectorant properties. The bioactive constituents of Didelta cernua include sesquiterpene lactones, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Didelta cernua include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to alleviate symptoms of colds, coughs, and respiratory infections.
